buyer_views.answer_contract: Return entitlements by identity, namespace, access mode, risk tier, lease TTL, review cadence, owner, reviewer pool, source evidence, and MCP tools needed for enforcement.
buyer_views.question: Can IAM see every agent identity, scope, owner, lease, reviewer, and revocation path?
buyer_views.title: IAM and non-human identity review

buyer_views.answer_contract: Evaluate identity_id, workflow_id, namespace, requested access mode, lease status, expiry, review status, authorization decision, approval evidence, and kill indicators before forwarding the call.
buyer_views.question: Can an MCP gateway decide whether a specific tool call still has active authority?
buyer_views.title: MCP gateway enforcement review

buyer_views.answer_contract: Show expiring entitlements, deterministic review decisions, standards alignment, source hashes, runtime evidence fields, and commercialization path for hosted access review APIs.
buyer_views.question: Does the project show a credible enterprise control plane beyond static prompts?
buyer_views.title: Acquisition diligence review

commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI coding platform, or cloud security vendor gets the agent-access lifecycle surface required to govern fleets of autonomous non-human identities.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Sell hosted entitlement lease issuance, continuous access review, IdP and SIEM integrations, revocation webhooks, step-up approval receipts, and MCP gateway enforcement APIs.
commercialization_path.open_layer: Publish the generated entitlement pack, deterministic evaluator, MCP tools, and docs so adopters can model agent access reviews without buying a platform first.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Support customer-private entitlement profiles, source-host reviewer adapters, IdP group mappings, MCP namespace approvals, and access-review exports.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: The project can prove which agent identity has which scope right now, when that authority expires, who must review it, and what kills the session.
default_questions_answered: Which MCP namespaces and access modes does each identity hold?
Which entitlements require approval or step-up authorization?
When should access be allowed, held, denied, or killed?
Which evidence packs and MCP tools explain the decision?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the pack to quarterly agent-access reviews, MCP gateway design, AI platform intake, high-impact action approvals, and procurement or acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open entitlement evidence, then sell hosted permission leases, access-review automation, IdP adapters, approval receipts, and MCP gateway enforcement APIs.

executive_readout.board_level_claim: The project can prove which agent identity has which scope right now, when that authority expires, who must review it, and what kills the session.
executive_readout.headline: SecurityRecipes now treats agent authority as an expiring entitlement, not a static permission.
executive_readout.recommended_first_use: Attach the pack to quarterly agent-access reviews, MCP gateway design, AI platform intake, high-impact action approvals, and procurement or acquisition diligence.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, IAM, Security Engineering, Product Security, GRC, SOC, procurement security, model-risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI entitlement review and permission lease governance
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can turn static agent identity contracts into expiring, reviewable, revocable entitlements so agent hosts and MCP gateways know whether a specific identity still has authority to use a scope right now.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI is becoming a fleet problem. Enterprises need the same lifecycle discipline they expect for privileged human and workload identities, but adapted for autonomous agents that use MCP tools, receive secure context, delegate across A2A-style protocols, persist memory, and trigger action-runtime gates.

residual_risks.risk: The open pack models entitlements and decisions, but production enforcement still needs IdP, MCP gateway, approval-system, and agent-host integration.
residual_risks.treatment: Bind runtime credentials to entitlement_id, lease_id, run_id, tenant_id, authorization decision, approval records, and receipt evidence before forwarding MCP calls.

residual_risks.risk: Static leases can drift from real customer IAM or source-host state.
residual_risks.treatment: Use hosted evidence ingestion and revocation webhooks to reconcile IdP groups, service principals, source-host permissions, and MCP scopes continuously.

residual_risks.risk: Human access reviews can miss model, connector, and context drift.
residual_risks.treatment: Trigger entitlement review after model upgrades, connector drift, Agent Card changes, incident replay, new action classes, or standards-crosswalk changes.

decisions.meaning: The requested namespace and access mode are registered, the lease is active and unexpired, review is current, linked authorization allows the request, and no kill signal is present.
The entitlement exists, but review cadence, approval evidence, owner sponsorship, or privileged access confirmation is due before the agent can continue.
The entitlement exists, but the requested access needs fresh MCP authorization, scope challenge handling, human approval, or elevated policy evidence.
The identity, workflow, namespace, or requested access mode is not declared in the generated entitlement pack.
The entitlement lease is absent, expired, malformed, or not bound to the requested identity and namespace.
A revocation, token passthrough, secret exposure, identity-after-revocation, cross-tenant scope, or repeated denied action requires immediate session shutdown.

runtime_policy.lease_binding: Each runtime credential must bind identity_id, workflow_id, namespace, access mode, run_id, tenant_id, lease_id, expiry, and correlation_id.

standards_alignment.coverage: Maps entitlement review to identity and privilege abuse, tool misuse, insecure inter-agent communication, agentic supply-chain risk, cascading failures, and rogue-agent containment.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
